what_was_done: Added responsive HashDisplay component + ErrorBoundary with retry. Connected explorer to live TORUSd daemon (fixed creds, StakingInfo field name). All 5 API routes verified against live chain at height 2000.
known_issues: StakeMiner requires vNodes.size() >= 3 to activate - blocks solo/small-network staking
MAX_MONEY set to 200M - needs hard-fork bump before ~2034 (5% annual PoS growth)
Gate 3 blocks git push from Claude - user must push manually
pnSeed hardcoded seed is VPS own IP (circular) - add real peer IPs when network grows
torus-explorer address page limited in v1 - no address index in daemon RPC
torus-explorer dev server uses port 3001 (3000 occupied by toroidal-economy app)
next_steps: Lower vNodes.size() < 3 requirement in miner.cpp:542 to allow staking with fewer peers
Verify v2.0.2 release CI completes (Docker push to ghcr.io/ozmasterai/torusd)
torus-explorer: visual QA of responsive hash truncation in browser
torus-explorer: add pagination to blocks list page
torus-explorer: add WebSocket or polling for live block updates
last_response_preview:
Yes, exactly — `~/.claude` IS being counted as a project. Here's the smoking gun:

**`hooks/boot_pkg/util.py:103-104`**
```python
while d != os.sep and d != home:
    if d == claude_dir:
        return "torus-framework", claude_dir, None, None  # <-- THIS
```

The fallback git-ancestor walk explicitly returns `"torus-framework"` when it hits `~/.claude`. That makes `_is_project_session = True` in orchestrator.py, which then reads `session_count` from `.claude-state.json` (24) instead of `LIVE_ST
